content: If we borrow the linguistic definition of reflexion as both 'physical reflection of light' and 'deliberate cognitive reflection' from high school English vocabulary studies, we can reframe LLM self-reflection as a dual-layer optical-cognitive system, where inconsistent solving perspectives from existing LLM reflection methods act as different angled light sources to cast clearer multi-dimensional shadows of a problem, rather than just a single flat cognitive self-review.
